Bot not replying Not registered error Format errors Wrong category Wrong GBP amount No exchange rate Receipt problems Budget warnings

Can't log in No expenses visible Wrong budget percentage

WhatsApp not working Telegram not working Duplicate expenses Exchange rate not applying Celery worker down Sentry noise

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and how to resolve them · For all user roles

Caretaker Issues

Bot not replying to my message

Possible CauseSolution
Message wasn't delivered (network issue)Check you have internet. Wait 1 minute and resend.
Celery worker is down (system issue)Contact your site manager, who should escalate to the UK admin.
Bot number/username is wrongVerify you're messaging the correct bot contact.
WhatsApp 24-hour window expiredSend a new message to re-open the conversation window.
Tip If you see a single tick (WhatsApp) or no reply after 2 minutes, the message may not have reached the server. Check your internet connection and resend.

Getting "not registered" error

This means your phone number (WhatsApp) or Telegram username is not linked to a user account in the system.

1
Contact your site manager
2
They will ask a UK admin to add/correct your phone number or Telegram username
3
Once updated, try sending your expense again
For Admins WhatsApp: Phone must be in E.164 format without the + prefix (e.g. 256701234567).
Telegram: Username must be entered without the @ symbol (e.g. john_kampala).

Getting format error messages

The bot replied with:

Send expenses as:
Category Amount [description]

Example: Food 50000 rice Kalerwe

Common causes:

What you sentProblemFix
50000 riceCategory missingFood 50000 rice
Food fifty thousandAmount not a numberFood 50000
Food $50000Currency symbol includedFood 50000
rice Kalerwe 50000 FoodWrong orderFood 50000 rice Kalerwe
Food 5e4Scientific notationFood 50000
Food -50000Negative amountFood 50000
Food 0Zero amountEnter the actual amount spent
Remember the format Category Amount Description — always in this order. Category first, then the number, then optional description.

Category not recognised or wrong category matched

If you see:

Category 'Fuud' not recognised.
Valid categories: Clothing, Contingency, Education, Food, Maintenance,
Medical, Renovations, Salaries, Transportation, Utilities

Your spelling was too far from any known category. Resend with the exact name from the list above.

If your expense was filed under the wrong category (e.g. "Fooding" matched "Clothing"), contact your site manager to have the UK admin correct it in the system.

Amount converted to wrong GBP value

The GBP value depends on the exchange rate stored in the system. If it looks wrong:

  • The exchange rate may be outdated — ask your admin to update it
  • You may have entered the amount in the wrong currency — always use local currency
  • The admin can edit the expense manually if needed

Getting "No exchange rate found" error

This means no exchange rate is configured for your site's currency. Contact your admin immediately — they need to add an exchange rate under Expenses → Exchange rates before you can log expenses.

Receipt photo not attached

IssueSolution
Sent photo as a separate messageAttach the photo and put the expense text in the caption field, not as a separate message
Photo too largeTake a lower-resolution photo or crop to just the receipt
Sent a document instead of photoSend as a photo (from camera/gallery), not as a file
Confirmation says "Receipt: none"The caption was read but the image wasn't processed — resend with the photo properly attached

What does the budget warning mean?

Budget warnings appear when a category's spending reaches certain thresholds:

WarningMeaningAction
Budget alert: 80%+Spending is approaching the annual limitInform your site manager. Be mindful of future spending in this category.
BUDGET EXCEEDED: 100%+The annual budget has been exceededInform your site manager immediately. They will liaise with the UK admin.
Your expense is still saved Budget warnings are alerts only — they do NOT block your expense. The expense is always recorded.

Site Manager Issues

Can't log in to the admin panel

CauseSolution
Wrong passwordAsk a UK admin to reset your password
Account not activeAsk a UK admin to check the "Active" checkbox on your user account
Staff status not setAsk a UK admin to check the "Staff status" checkbox
Google SSO not workingEnsure you're using a @ccdawah.org email. Other domains are not permitted.
Browser issueClear cookies, try incognito mode, or try a different browser

No expenses showing for my site

  • Check the date filter — you may be looking at the wrong month/year
  • Check the site filter — make sure your site is selected
  • If no expenses exist yet, your caretakers may not have started sending them
  • If caretakers say they've sent messages but nothing appears, the Celery worker may be down — contact the UK admin

Budget percentage looks wrong

Budget calculations only count expenses with "logged" or "reviewed" status. Expenses marked as "queried" are excluded. This is by design — queried expenses are under investigation and shouldn't count toward the budget until resolved.

Admin Issues

No expenses appearing from WhatsApp

1
Check Celery is running: SSH into the Celery server and run systemctl status celery
2
Check webhook config: In Meta Developer Console, verify the webhook URL points to /webhooks/whatsapp/ and the "messages" field is subscribed
3
Check message logs: Go to Messaging → WhatsApp Messages. If messages appear here but no expenses are created, the parsing may have failed — check Sentry for errors
4
Check environment variables: Verify WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID, and WHATSAPP_APP_SECRET are set correctly

No expenses appearing from Telegram

1
Check Celery is running (same as above)
2
Check webhook registration: Verify the Telegram webhook is registered by calling https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getWebhookInfo
3
Check message logs: Go to Messaging → Telegram Messages
4
Check environment variables: Verify TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET

Duplicate expenses appearing

The system has 3-layer deduplication (Redis, DB check in view, Celery task). Duplicates are very rare, but can occur if:

  • A Celery task was manually retried (e.g. via Flower)
  • Redis was restarted while messages were in flight

Fix: Delete the duplicate expense from the admin. Check if the issue is recurring — if so, investigate Redis connectivity.

Exchange rate not applying correctly

The system uses the most recent rate on or before the expense date. If the wrong rate is applied:

  • Check that a rate exists for the correct currency pair with an effective date on or before the expense date
  • If you've just added a rate, it applies to future expenses only — existing expenses keep their original rate
  • You can manually edit an expense's GBP amount if needed

Celery worker appears to be down

1
SSH into the Celery server
2
Check status: systemctl status celery
3
If stopped, restart: sudo systemctl restart celery
4
Check logs: journalctl -u celery -f
5
Verify Redis is also running: redis-cli ping should return PONG
Messages are not lost While Celery is down, webhook messages are still received and stored in the database (WhatsApp/Telegram incoming messages). Once Celery is restarted, queued tasks will be processed. However, if Redis was also down, the task queue may have been lost — check message logs for any that weren't processed.

DisallowedHost errors in Sentry

Errors like "Invalid HTTP_HOST header: 'random-domain.com'" are caused by bots scanning your server's IP address with random hostnames. These are harmless — Django is correctly rejecting them.

To reduce noise: You can filter out DisallowedHost errors in your Sentry configuration. This is cosmetic only — no action is required.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I send multiple expenses in one message?

No. Send one expense per message. Each message creates one expense record. If you need to log multiple expenses, send them as separate messages.

What happens if I send an expense on the wrong date?

The expense is recorded with the date the message was received. If you need to backdate an expense, contact your admin who can edit the expense date in the admin panel.

Can I delete or edit an expense I sent?

Caretakers cannot edit or delete expenses. Contact your site manager, who will ask a UK admin to make the correction.

What currencies are supported?

The system supports any currency for which an exchange rate has been configured. Currently active: UGX (Uganda), GMD (Gambia), IDR (Indonesia). New currencies can be added by creating exchange rate records.

How often should exchange rates be updated?

Recommended: weekly for volatile currencies, monthly for stable ones. The system uses automatic daily updates when an API key is configured, but manual updates are also supported.

Is there a limit on the expense amount?

Yes. The system rejects amounts that are zero, negative, in scientific notation, or greater than 100,000,000 (100 million) in local currency. This prevents data entry errors.

What happens if the system goes down?

Messages sent while the system is down may not receive a confirmation reply. However, if the webhook endpoint is still receiving requests (even if Celery is down), messages are stored in the database and will be processed when service is restored. If the entire server is unreachable, messages will fail to deliver — caretakers should keep a paper record and resend when service is restored.

Can caretakers see the admin dashboard?

No. The admin panel and reports dashboard require a login with "Staff" status. Caretakers only interact via WhatsApp or Telegram.

How do I export data?

Most admin list views support export via the Import/Export buttons. You can export to CSV or Excel format. The Audit Log also supports export for compliance purposes.

Who can I contact for technical support?

For system issues (server, webhooks, database), contact the UK technical team. For operational issues (budgets, users, categories), contact the UK finance admin. For on-the-ground issues (caretaker problems, format questions), the site manager is the first point of contact.

{% endblock %}
